content.problemStatement:
You are given an integer sequence A1,A2,ÔÇŽ,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1ÔÇő,A2ÔÇő,ÔÇŽ,ANÔÇő. For any pair of integers (l,r)(l, r)(l,r) such that 1ÔëĄlÔëĄrÔëĄN1 \le l \le r \le N1ÔëĄlÔëĄrÔëĄN, let's define OR(l,r)\mathrm{OR}(l, r)OR(l,r) as AlÔłĘAl+1ÔłĘÔÇŽÔłĘArA_l \lor A_{l+1} \lor \ldots \lor A_rAlÔÇőÔłĘAl+1ÔÇőÔłĘÔÇŽÔłĘArÔÇő. Here, ÔłĘ\lorÔłĘ is the bitwise OR operator.

In total, there are N(N+1)2\frac{N(N+1)}{2}2N(N+1)ÔÇő possible pairs (l,r)(l, r)(l,r), i.e. N(N+1)2\frac{N(N+1)}{2}2N(N+1)ÔÇő possible values of OR(l,r)\mathrm{OR}(l, r)OR(l,r). Determine if all these values are pairwise distinct.
constraints: the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 3Ôőů1053 \cdot 10^53Ôőů105
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,ÔÇŽ,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1ÔÇő,A2ÔÇő,ÔÇŽ,ANÔÇő.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing the string `"YES"` if all values of OR(l,r)\mathrm{OR}(l, r)OR(l,r) are pairwise distinct or `"NO"` otherwise (without quotes).

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: The values of OR(l,r)\mathrm{OR}(l, r)OR(l,r) are 1,2,7,3,7,71, 2, 7, 3, 7, 71,2,7,3,7,7 (corresponding to the contiguous subsequences [1],[2],[7],[1,2],[2,7],[1,2,7][1], [2], [7], [1,2], [2,7], [1,2,7][1],[2],[7],[1,2],[2,7],[1,2,7] respectively). We can see that these values are not pairwise distinct.
Example case 2: The values of OR(l,r)\mathrm{OR}(l, r)OR(l,r) are 1,2,31, 2, 31,2,3 (corresponding to the contiguous subsequences [1],[2],[1,2][1], [2], [1, 2][1],[2],[1,2] respectively) and they are pairwise distinct.
